content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO,95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
107 SOUTH BROADWAY • • • SUITE 2140A • • •     LOS ANGELES,9001.2

August 1, 1977


Wayne Lemieux
Attorney at Law
Post Office Box 1666
Studio City, California 91604

Dear Mr. Lemieux:

     This letter is to confirm my advice to you by telephone on
July 29, 1977, regarding with whom campaign statements of the
San Gabriel Valley Municipal Water District Directors should
be filed.

     You stated that Water District is composed of four uncontigious cities, and that several of the directors are elected
from entirely within one city, while others are voted upon
in more than one city. You asked whether campaign statements
of directors who are elected by the voters of single cities
should file with the respective city clerks as Government
Code Section 81005(d) would seem to indicate, while the others
should file with the Los Angeles Registrar of Voters pursuant
to Government Code Section 81005(e). I advised that in this
unusual situation, our office believes that for the convenience
of members of the public who may wish to view the statements,
the reports of all candidates or officeholders of the district
should be filed in one location, Los Angeles Registrar of
Voters' office.

     If our staff may be of further assistance to you, please
contact us.

                                        Sincerely yours,

                                        Nan Emmerson
                                        Nan Emmerson
                                        Special Compliance Representative


NE:jg


SACRAMENTO (916) 322-5660  •    LOS ANGELES (213) 620-5196

sections.question: Whether campaign statements of San Gabriel Valley Municipal Water District Directors who are elected by voters of single cities should file with respective city clerks under Government Code Section 81005(d), while others should file with the Los Angeles Registrar of Voters pursuant to Government Code Section 81005(e).
sections.conclusion: In this unusual situation, our office believes that for the convenience of members of the public who may wish to view the statements, the reports of all candidates or officeholders of the district should be filed in one location, Los Angeles Registrar of Voters' office.
sections.question_synthetic: Where should campaign statements be filed for directors of a multi-city water district, where some directors are elected from single cities and others are elected from multiple cities?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: All campaign statements for the water district's directors should be filed in one location at the Los Angeles Registrar of Voters' office for public convenience, regardless of whether directors are elected from single or multiple cities.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a confirmation of prior telephone advice. Question and conclusion sections are paraphrased from body text rather than appearing under formal headers. Letter is dated August 1, 1977 and signed by Nan Emmerson, Special Compliance Representative.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether campaign statements of San Gabriel Valley Municipal Water District Directors who are elected by voters of single cities should file with respective city clerks under Government Code Section 81005(d), while others should file with the Los Angeles Registrar of Voters pursuant to Government Code Section 81005(e).

CONCLUSION: In this unusual situation, our office believes that for the convenience of members of the public who may wish to view the statements, the reports of all candidates or officeholders of the district should be filed in one location, Los Angeles Registrar of Voters' office.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter confirming telephone guidance to an attorney regarding the proper filing location for campaign statements of San Gabriel Valley Municipal Water District Directors, recommending centralized filing with the Los Angeles Registrar of Voters for administrative convenience.
